text.title: JUnit 5 + Spring Boot Test Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to produce a right-sized Spring Boot test class.

step.name: pick-scope
step.action: Controller → controller scope. Service with collaborators → service scope. Repository/EntityManager → repository scope. Multi-layer end-to-end → integration scope.

step.name: annotate-correctly
step.action: Controller: `@WebMvcTest(X.class)` + `@MockBean` for services. Service: `@ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class)` + `@Mock` + `@InjectMocks`. Repository: `@DataJpaTest` + Testcontainers Postgres if dialect matters. Integration: `@SpringBootTest(webEnvironment=RANDOM_PORT) + @Testcontainers + @Container`.
step.decision-gate: If `@SpringBootTest` chosen for controller scope, FAIL.

step.name: write-test-methods
step.action: Name methods `methodUnderTest_input_expected`. Use AssertJ `assertThat(...)`. Async tests return `Mono`/`Flux`/`CompletableFuture` or use `Awaitility`.

step.name: coverage-and-ci
step.action: Enable JaCoCo with a sensible threshold (70-85%). Fail CI on coverage drop. Run mutation testing (Pitest) optionally for critical packages.
